theme: A Hot-Air Balloon Festival in April
text:
Certainly the most romantic event of our year is the ballooning festival held in April, nearly at the foot of the mountain. To be there at sunrise, when the first balloon lifts and the music of a brass band drifts over the field, is to feel a zest for life that no trivial worry can deprive. I will reply at once to anyone who calls it a freak hobby: they simply have not tried it.

My uncle, a grown-up who never lost his boyish wonder, taught me the trade. He would shave at dawn, light his pipe of tobacco, and forecast the weather with an accuracy the local viewer of the weekly broadcast could not match. "The wind is erratic today," he might gasp, half out of breath, "so we adjust the diet of fuel and watch the gauge." His anatomy of the sport was thorough, almost clinical, and his yearly notes filled a thick file.

Conversely, my aunt was inefficient with the ropes but a genius with people. She would console a nervous first-timer, hush a crying child, and offer popcorn and a glass of champagne to anyone who looked pale. Her zest for hospitality gave the festival its prominence. She disliked any deception; a salesman who once tried a sly back-call to upsell cheap gear got a mute, distinctly cold reply, and she sent him off with a limp wave and a yawn.

The science is real, though laymen find it enlightening. Hot air, being neutral and light, expands and the craft rises; cool it, and you descend. A periodic check of the network of valves is mandatory. One must never underestimate the strain on the fabric, nor the threat of a sudden gust. A humanitarian group even uses balloons to extend aid to flooded country districts, dropping a supplementary parcel of food where a bomber could never safely fly.

This year a contemporary artist, a luxurious dreamer with a fortune to spend, painted his balloon to imply the migration of the locust and the grasshopper across the plain, a strange but striking image. A clinical psychologist gave a talk on the temptation to overwork and the suffering it breeds; she urged us, economically and otherwise, to rest. Even the Egyptian consul, a descendant of an ancient line, came to acclaim the show, and an old Swede in a tan jacket wept with quiet pity at its beauty.

As the climax neared, fifty balloons rose at once. A volley of cheers went up. The performance was flawless. I felt my own anguish of the past year drain away. To imply that such joy is mere escape would be to misread it; it is, rather, a phase of healing. Whatever doom the headlines forecast, here, for a few apiece-bought hours, we were free. The literature of flight, I thought, is written not in books but in the slow, ginger ascent of a single bright sphere into the morning sky, and I would borrow that feeling for as long as I could, fully and without apology, before the limp ropes called me back to earth and sleep.

If I must give a brief synopsis of why this endeavor matters, it is this. The festival's success rate is no accident; we accommodate every type of visitor, from the child who points at a platypus drawn on a banner to the retiree living on a modest annuity. Volunteers work overtime without complaint, and any variance in the schedule is posted early so no one need disagree at the gate. We expand the program each year, replacing every dead bulb in the night display and refusing to let litter contaminate the field. The crew that runs the propane will not trigger a single careless spark; safety is the prime ingredient of the whole affair. No one is dragged off to jail for a mistake, but no one cuts a corner either.
translation:
我们这一年里最浪漫的活动，无疑是四月间几乎在山脚下举行的热气球节。在日出时分到场，当第一只气球升起、铜管乐队的音乐飘过原野时，你会感到一种对生活的热情，是任何琐碎烦恼都无法夺走的。对任何把它称作古怪嗜好的人，我会立刻回应：他们只是没尝试过罢了。

我叔叔是个从未失去孩童般好奇心的成年人，是他把这门手艺教给我的。他会在黎明刮胡子，点上一斗烟草，预报天气的准确度连每周收看广播的本地观众都比不上。"今天的风变化无常，"他会气喘吁吁地说，"所以我们要调整燃料的'食量'，盯着仪表看。"他对这项运动的剖析十分透彻，几乎像临床分析，他每年的笔记装满了厚厚一摞文件。

相反，我婶婶摆弄绳索很笨拙，可与人打交道却是个天才。她会安慰紧张的新手，哄住啼哭的孩子，给任何脸色发白的人递上爆米花和一杯香槟。她待客的热情让这个节庆声名远扬。她讨厌任何欺骗；曾有个推销员想用狡猾的二次回访来推销廉价装备，得到的是一个无声、明显冷淡的答复，她对他懒懒地挥挥手、打个呵欠，就把他打发走了。

其中的科学是真实的，尽管外行人觉得颇受启发。热空气呈中性且轻盈，受热膨胀，飞行器便上升；让它冷却，你就下降。对那一整套阀门网络进行周期性检查是强制规定。人绝不能低估织物所承受的拉力，也不能低估突发阵风的威胁。一个人道主义团体甚至用气球把援助延伸到被洪水淹没的乡村地区，把额外的一包食物投放到轰炸机永远无法安全飞抵的地方。

今年，一位当代艺术家——一个挥金如土、爱好奢华的梦想家——把他的气球画成暗示蝗虫和蚂蚱跨越平原迁徙的图案，怪异却引人注目。一位临床心理学家做了演讲，谈到过度工作的诱惑及其滋生的苦难；她敦促我们无论从节俭还是其他角度都要休息。连那位古老家族的后裔、埃及领事也来为演出喝彩，一位穿棕褐色夹克的老瑞典人则带着安静的怜惜为它的美而落泪。

临近高潮时，五十只气球同时升空。一阵欢呼齐声爆发。表演完美无缺。我感到过去一年自身的痛苦渐渐流走。若把这种喜悦仅仅说成逃避，那是误读了它；它毋宁是疗愈的一个阶段。无论报纸头条预报了怎样的厄运，在这里，在花钱买来的、每人各享的几个钟头里，我们是自由的。我想，飞行的文学并不写在书本里，而写在一只明亮的球体缓慢、谨慎地升入晨空的过程中，我会尽可能久地借用那种感觉，毫无保留，毫不道歉，直到松垂的绳索把我唤回大地与睡眠。

如果我必须简要概述这项事业为何重要，那就是这样。节庆的成功率绝非偶然；我们容纳每一类访客，从指着横幅上画的鸭嘴兽的孩子，到靠微薄年金生活的退休者。志愿者们毫无怨言地加班，日程上任何变动都会提早公布，免得有人在入口处不一致而起争执。我们每年扩展项目，更换夜间灯光秀里每一只坏掉的灯泡，绝不让垃圾污染场地。负责丙烷的工作组不会触发任何一星半点粗心的火花；安全是整个活动最首要的成分。没人会因一个失误被拖进监狱，但也没人偷工减料。
